title: KitchenKit
tagline: Recipe management and prep intelligence for professional kitchens.
description: KitchenKit is a recipe management and kitchen intelligence platform built for professional use — scaling recipes by ratio, generating prep task queues from production volume, tracking pantry levels in real time, and exposing everything as an MCP server so CulinaryOS can access the full recipe and prep layer as part of its AI-agent workflows.
story: Built to solve the recipe scaling problem properly — not by multiplying ingredient quantities, but by preserving ratios so the dish still works at any volume. Once that core was solid, the prep queue and pantry layers followed naturally.
journey: KitchenKit started as the recipe scaling module I kept wanting inside CulinaryOS but needed to build and validate standalone first. The ratio engine was the hard part — most recipe tools just multiply quantities, which breaks the dish at scale. Getting ratios right meant the prep queue and pantry modules almost wrote themselves, and the MCP layer let CulinaryOS use all of it without building a second system.
forWho: Head chefs, sous chefs, and kitchen managers who need recipe scaling that actually works, prep planning that accounts for real production volume, and pantry tracking that stays in sync.

detailedSpecs.architecture: pnpm monorepo managed by Turborepo. React 18 + Vite web app backed by Supabase (PostgreSQL + RLS + RPC functions). The kitchenkit-mcp server runs alongside the web app, exposing recipe, prep, and pantry data as MCP tools for CulinaryOS agent workflows.
detailedSpecs.dataFlow: Recipe stored as ratio blueprint → production volume entered → ratio-engine scales ingredients → prep task queue generated → pantry levels decremented on completion → MCP tools expose all layers to CulinaryOS.
keyPatterns: Ratio Blueprint Engine — shared with CulinaryOS, recipes as ratios not fixed quantities
kitchenkit-mcp server — MCP interface for CulinaryOS hub integration
Prep queue generation from production volume targets
Real-time pantry tracking via Supabase Realtime
Shared auth with CulinaryOps (Supabase magic link)
detailedSpecs.deployTarget: Railway (standalone) or embedded via CulinaryOS MCP layer
